content.problemStatement:
You have an array AAA of size NNN, and a complete weighted graph with NNN nodes and N‚čÖ(N‚ąí1)2\frac{N \cdot (N-1)}{2}2N‚čÖ(N‚ąí1)‚Äč undirected weighted edges connecting each pair of nodes.

The weight of the edge between node iii and node jjj is ‚ą£j‚ąíi‚ą£‚čÖmax‚Ā°(Ai,Aj)|j - i| \cdot \max(A_i, A_j)‚ą£j‚ąíi‚ą£‚čÖmax(Ai‚Äč,Aj‚Äč).

The distance between 222 nodes is defined as the **minimum sum** of weights on a path connecting the 222 nodes.

Find the distance between nodes 111 and NNN.

Note that ‚ą£x‚ą£|x|‚ą£x‚ą£ denotes the absolute value of xxx. For example, ‚ą£‚ąí7‚ą£=7|-7| = 7‚ą£‚ąí7‚ą£=7 and ‚ą£4‚ą£=4|4| = 4‚ą£4‚ą£=4.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the number of nodes in the graph and the size of array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN integers, A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the distance between nodes 111 and NNN.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The optimal path is 1‚Üí21 \rightarrow 21‚Üí2 and then 2‚Üí42 \rightarrow 42‚Üí4 with sum of weights 2+2=42 + 2 = 42+2=4.
Test case 222: The optimal path is 1‚Üí21 \rightarrow 21‚Üí2 and then 2‚Üí32 \rightarrow 32‚Üí3 with sum of weights 3+2=53 + 2 = 53+2=5.
